data.title: SaaS provider Youxin Technology prices IPO at $4.50 per share
data.description:
• Youxin Technology (NASDAQ:YAAS) priced of its initial public offering of 2.3M shares at a public offering price of $4.50 per share.
  • Youxin Technology Ltd is a software as a service ((SaaS)) and platform as a service ((PaaS)) provider committed to helping retail enterprises digitally transform their businesses using its cloud-based SaaS product and PaaS platform to develop, use and control business applications without the need to purchase complex IT infrastructure.
  • The shares have been approved for listing on Nasdaq and are expected to commence trading on December 20, 2024 under the ticker symbol “YAAS.”
  • The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of $10.35M.
  • Underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to 345,000 additional shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 23, 2024.
  • Net proceeds will be used for research and development, including development of SaaS standard product and further investment in the company’s cloud services, (ii) investment in the company’s sales and marketing, including expanding distribution channels for existing and future market, and (iii) general corporate purposes, which may include capital expenditures, potential strategic investments and acquisitions.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
08:30 AM Personal Income and Outlays

Each month, the Bureau of Economic Analysis also compiles the personal consumption expenditure price index, also known as the PCE price index. This inflation index measures a basket of goods and services that is updated annually in contrast to the CPI, which measures a fixed basket. The November PCE price index is seen up 0.2 percent on the month and up 2.3 percent from a year ago. Core PCE is seen up 0.2 percent on the month and up 2.8 percent on the year.

10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment

The University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index rates the relative level of current and future economic conditions. There are two versions of this data released two weeks apart, preliminary and revised. The preliminary data tends to have a greater impact. The reading is compiled from a survey of around 500 consumers. The December preliminary data is expected to be 74.0, stable from 74.0 in November.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. The active rig count acts as a leading indicator of demand for oil products. Crude Oil Rigs in the United States were steady at 482 on December 13 week.Click here for the weekly economic calendar.

data.title: Nike's turnaround will take time and cost money, CEO says
data.description:
Under its new CEO, Elliott Hill, Nike (NYSE:NKE) is on a mission to restore the brand to its former glory by moving aggressively to move aged inventory, beefing up its digital presence, and limit promotional pricing. But these initiatives will take time and cost money, leading the sportswear giant to warn that the company faces headwinds to financials for the balance of the year and will leave 2025 summer order books down from last year.

“This will take time,” Hill said on his first earnings call as CEO, “But I’m confident we are making the right move.”

To re-energize the Nike (NKE) brand momentum, the company will take strategic actions that include shifting Nike Digital to a full-price model and reducing the percentage of the business driven by promotional activity. Some of these initiatives have already been in place, others will be accelerated.

“Nike has become far too promotional,” Hill added, and efforts to restore the company back to a full price business involves “cleaning up the marketplace, investing in the brand and in Nike Direct,” and increase spending on marketing.

“The net effect of these actions will result in lower revenue, additional gross margin pressure, and higher demand creation expenses with a greater headwind to the fourth quarter compared to the third quarter,” CFO Matthew Friend said on the call.

To that end, the company is projecting third quarter revenue to be down low double digits on the initiatives outlined earlier as well as on foreign exchange headwinds, with gross margin down 300-350 basis points.

For fiscal Q2, Nike (NKE) sales were down 8% with footwear revenue down 11% and apparel revenue declining 1%. Gross margin narrowed 100 basis points, while net income fell 26% year-over year. While the results were not encouraging, shares were jolted higher as the quarter was not as bad as Wall Street feared.

The outlook, however, spooked investors and sent shares back into the red despite Hill and Friend’s assurances that these initiatives will yield long-term benefits.

“I rejoined Nike to take our consumers, our amazing athletes and this great company to someplace new,” said Hill.

data.title: BlackBerry in charts: IoT revenue rises Y/Y while cybersecurity revenue drops in FQ3
Earnings Snapshot: BlackBerry FQ3 results top estimates, FY25 rev guidance misses consensus
Primis Financial reauthorizes stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Primis Financial (NASDAQ:FRST) said on Thursday that its board has approved a stock repurchase program for up to 740,600 of the outstanding shares of the company's common stock.
  • The stock repurchase program will begin on December 19, 2024, and conclude on December 19, 2025, subject to the earlier termination or extension of the stock repurchase program.
  • Under the program, the company may repurchase shares of common stock from time to time in open market purchases or privately negotiated transactions.
  • The company also announced today that, as expected, it received a decision from the Nasdaq Hearings Panel granting the company's request for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market, and confirming that the company has regained compliance.

data.title: Crown Castle gains on report it's close to $8B sale of fiber unit to TPG
data.description:
Private equity firm TPG Inc. (TPG) is in advanced discussions to acquire Crown Castle's (NYSE:CCI) fiber unit for about $8 billion. Shares of Crown Castle rose 7% in after hours trading.

A deal for the fiber and wireless assets may happen within weeks, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Nothing has been finalized, and the talks could still falter.

A potential sale comes as activist investor Elliott Investment Management has been pushing Crown Castle (CCI) to make changes, including evaluating the fiber unit, since late last year.

Reuters reported in early October that fiber network owner Zayo Group and private equity firm TPG (TPG) were competing to purchase fiber and wireless assets of Crown Castle (CCI) in a deal that may be valued at about $10 billion.

Elliott revealed in a letter last November that it had taken a more than $2 billion stake in Crown Castle (CCI) and was pushing for board changes and other moves at the owner of wireless towers.

data.title: Raytheon secures $282M U.S. Air Force contract
data.description:
• Raytheon (NYSE:RTX) has been awarded a $282.34M U.S. Air Force contract for Small Diameter Bomb Increment II, GBU-53/B Production Lot 11.
  • This contract provides for all up rounds and containers. Work will be performed in Tucson, Arizona, and is expected to be completed by March 19, 2029.
  • This contract was a sole source acquisition.
  • The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center, Eglin Air Force Base, Florida, is the contracting activity.
